response:
Yes — I pulled the latest parameters from `helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`.

**Quick dosing summary for Dr. Martinez**

| Patient | Weight (kg) | Dose (mg/kg) | Calculated dose (mg) | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance (mL/min) | Clinical note |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| CU-001 | 12.4 | 0.15 | 1.86 | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 | Stable; tumor regression 22% at last scan |
| CU-002 | 9.1 | 0.12 | 1.09 | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 | Responding well; weight gain improving |
| CU-003 | 15.7 | 0.18 | 2.83 | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 | New enrollment; first dose given 2026-03-27 |

**PK model status**
- Based on this latest dosing config, the PK-derived parameters look **internally consistent**.
- I don’t see any obvious dosing red flags in the current file.
- CU-003’s q2w schedule aligns correctly with the documented first dose date.

**Caveat:** this file is a parameter snapshot, not a live model-health log, so I can say the **current dosing outputs look stable**, but I can’t fully verify runtime/model-system stability from this file alone.

If you want, I can format this into a brief report for Dr. Martinez.
